functions.name: alamos.dev.instrumentation
documentation:
:returns: development instrumentation that connects to a locally hosted uptrace
server (see alamos/dev/README.md). It's best to only call this function once within
a testing environment, such as using a session scoped fixture in conftest.py.

functions.name: alamos.noop.noop

functions.name: alamos.environment.env_threshold_filter
documentation:
returns an environment filter that returns true if the environment is greater than
or equal to the threshold.

```
param threshold

The threshold environment to compare against
```

variables.name: alamos.environment.ENVIRONMENTS
documentation: List of valid environments

variables.name: alamos.environment.Environment
documentation:
Environment defines the environment in which instrumentation is running. Traces can
be constrained to run only in certain environments.

variables.name: alamos.environment.EnvironmentFilter
documentation:
EnvironmentFilter is a function takes a environment and returns true if the environment
is valid for use i.e. 'should this trace be executed?'

functions.name: alamos.instrumentation.trace
documentation:
Trace the given method on the class. This method can only be used on a class that
implements the Traceable protocol.

```
param key

The key of the span.

param env

The environment to run this span under. If the Tracer's environment
```

filter rejects the env, a no-op span is returned.

variables.name: alamos.instrumentation.NOOP
documentation:
Noop is instrumentation that does nothing. We highly recommend using this as a
default value for instrumentation fields or function arguments.

classes.name: alamos.instrumentation.Instrumentation

methods.name: alamos.instrumentation.Instrumentation.child
documentation:
Creates a child of this instrumentation with the given key.

```
param key

The key to set on the child. If the parent's path is "parent" and the
```

provided key is "child", the child's path will be "parent.child". We recommend
keeping this key unique within the children of the parent, as name conflicts
may cause unexpected behavior.
\:returns: A new child Instrumentation.

class_variables.name: alamos.instrumentation.Instrumentation.Meta
documentation: Metadata bout the instrumentation.

class_variables.name: alamos.instrumentation.Instrumentation.L
documentation: The logger for this instrumentation.

class_variables.name: alamos.instrumentation.Instrumentation.T
documentation: The tracer for this instrumentation.

Instrumentation is alamos' core data type, and represents a collection of
instrumentation tools: a logger and a tracer.

Instrumentation is specifically designed for dependency injection into different
services within your application. We recommend creating a single Instrumentation
instance per application and passing it around to different services.

Instrumentation is also focused on making noop functionality as easy as possible.
For that, we've provided the NOOP constant, which we recommend using as the default
value in function or constructor arguments. This allows you to inject preconfigured
instrumentation when you want to, and simply default to noop when you don't.

Instrumentation is organized as a hierarchy, where the child method is used to create
instrumentation that extends the key of its parent. This allows for your logs and
traces to match the architecture of your application. For example, instrumentation
that tracks low-level db requests could be created with the key child("db") and
instrumentation that tracks api requests could be created with the key child("api").
See the child method for more details.

classes.name: alamos.instrumentation.Traceable
documentation:
A protocol for classes whose methods can be traced using the trace
decorator

variables.name: alamos.trace.NOOP_TRACER
documentation: A tracer that does nothing.

classes.name: alamos.trace.Span

methods.name: alamos.trace.Span.record_exception
documentation:
If exception is not none, records it on the span and sets the span's status
to error.

```
param exc

An optionally defined exception to record.
```

class_variables.name: alamos.trace.Span.key
documentation:
The key identifying the span. This is the name of the key passed into 'trace'
combined with the path of the instrumentation that started the span. For example,
take instrumentation titled 'synnax' and a call to trace with 'test'. The span key
would be 'synnax.test'
A protocol for a Span that is part of a trace.

classes.name: alamos.trace._Span
documentation: Base implementation of the Span protocol

classes.name: alamos.trace.NoopSpan
documentation: Span that does nothing.

classes.name: alamos.trace.Tracer

methods.name: alamos.trace.Tracer.trace
documentation:
Starts a new span with the given key and environment. If
a span already exists on the current context, the new span is made as its child.

```
param key

The key of the span.

param env

The environment to run the span under.

return

A span that tracks program execution. If the Tracer's environment filter
```

rejects the provided env or the Tracer is noop, a no-op span is provided.

methods.name: alamos.trace.Tracer.debug
documentation:
Starts a new span at the 'debug' level. If a span already exists on the current
context, the new span is made as its child.

```
param key

The key of the span.

return

A span that tracks program execution. If the Tracer's environment filter
```

rejects the 'debug' env or the Tracer is noop, a no-op span is provided.

methods.name: alamos.trace.Tracer.bench
documentation:
Starts a new span at the 'debug' level. If a span already exists on the current
context, the new span is made as its child.

```
param key

The key of the span.

return

A span that tracks program execution. If the Tracer's environment filter
```

rejects the 'bench' env or the Tracer is noop, a no-op span is provided.

methods.name: alamos.trace.Tracer.prod
documentation:
Starts a new span at the 'debug' level. If a span already exists on the current
context, the new span is made as its child.

```
param key

The key of the span.

return

A span that tracks program execution. If the Tracer's environment filter
```

rejects the 'prod' env or the Tracer is noop, a no-op span is provided.

methods.name: alamos.trace.Tracer.propagate
documentation:
Injects metadata about the current trace into the provided carrier.
This metadata can be parsed on the other side of a network or IPC request using
allowing the trace to propagate across services.

```
param carrier

The carrier to set the trace metadata on.
```

Tracer wraps an open-telemetry tracer to provide an opinionated interface for
tracing within the Synnax stack.
